learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate the ability to work effectively and collaboratively within professional teams (Speech Pathology Australia Professional Standards (2020) (SPA PS) Domains/Standards 1.4, 2.3, 2.5, 2.6, 3.1, 3.3, 3.4).

Apply the principles of person-, family-, and community-centred care, together with interprofessional practice, to the delivery of culturally responsive and evidence-based speech pathology practice (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.4, 1.5, 1.6, 1.7, 2.5, 2.6, 3.1, 3.2, 3.4, 3.5).

Analyse, evaluate, and respond to ethical issues encountered in speech pathology practice using relevant professional, ethical, and legal frameworks (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 2.1 – 2.5).

Critically reflect on your future role within the evolving speech pathology profession and identify strategies to support, advocate for, and contribute to the profession and the communities it serves (SPA PS Domains/Standards 2.1 – 2.7).

Evaluate and develop approaches to lifelong learning, professional development, supervision, and reflective practice to support ongoing competence and professional growth throughout your career (SPA PS Domains/Standards 2.1 – 2.7).

assessments.due_date:
Week 8, Fri 1:00 pm

Week 13, Fri 1:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
Students will complete two (2) individual audiovisual reflections during the semester that explore the development of their professional identity as emerging speech pathologists. The reflections provide an opportunity to critically examine personal values, professional growth, and evolving understanding of ethical practice, advocacy, and professional responsibilities. Reflection 1 (due Week 8) focuses on students’ emerging professional identity at the commencement of the course, while Reflection 2 (due Week 13) requires students to revisit and critically reflect on how their thinking has developed over the semester as they prepare to transition into professional practice. Reflections are assessed on a pass/fail basis and both tasks must be passed to successfully complete this assessment.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Professional reflections will be submitted through the SPCH7223 eLearning Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
6/10/2026 10:00 am

The exam will be held during class time.
assessments.task_description:
Two (2) ethical issues will be presented. Using the Speech Pathology Australia ethical decision-making model, students will be asked to state the facts of the case, identify if there is a problem that requires action and describe the ethical issue, their proposed decision and action plan, and their evaluation plan. Students will be required to use relevant legislation, codes of ethics, etc to justify their actions.

This examination must be passed for the student to obtain a passing grade for the course.

This examination will be an in-person on-campus School based In-semester examination via Inspera. Students will be provided with the two (2) ethical issues 24 hours prior to the exam and will have access to the Speech Pathology Australia Code of Ethics during the examination. 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
20/10/2026

Presentations will occur during class time between 10am and 2pm.
assessments.task_description:
Speech pathologists are committed to lifelong learning and ongoing professional development. In acknowledgement of our obligation to engage in learning with colleagues, students, and the community through participation in professional development, students will prepare and deliver a 20-minute group seminar presentation providing an overview and evaluation of a professional development event suitable for a new graduate speech pathologist. Student groups will be assigned a specific workplace and a hypothetical professional development "budget" of $1500.

Students will be marked on both the quality, content, and delivery of their presentation. A detailed task description and marking rubric will be available on the SPCH7223 eLearning Blackboard site.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
This is the final semester of the speech pathology program and students are about to transition to professional practice. A critical skill of a speech pathologist is advocacy for their profession, themselves and their clients. Advocacy is defined as “public support for or recommendation of a particular cause or policy”.

This individual assignment requires students to reflect on what advocacy means to them. Specifically, students will address the following questions:

  1. How would you demonstrate advocacy for the profession of speech pathology as a whole and why is this important?
  2. How would you demonstrate advocacy for yourself as a speech pathologist and why is this important?
  3. How would you as a speech pathologist demonstrate advocacy for the importance of person-, family-, and community-centred care and why is this important?


Students are required to submit an audiovisual zoom recording addressing the above three (3) questions. This recording is to be 8-10 minutes in length and will be submitted through the SPCH7223 eLearning Blackboard site. Students will present their recording in association with a 1-slide electronic poster/PowerPoint slide outlining the key information they are presenting, and a copy of their script for this presentation.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Audiovisual recordings will be submitted through the SPCH7223 eLearning Blackboard site.
